content:
# elizaos/eliza Weekly Report (May 31 - 6, 2026)

## 🚀 Highlights
The week of May 31 to June 6, 2026, was defined by a major push toward infrastructure hardening and the maturation of the Eliza Cloud ecosystem. Development focused on stabilizing agent provisioning, implementing robust metered billing, and refining the multi-agent orchestrator to improve reliability in complex tasks. Significant progress was made in enhancing the developer experience through desktop startup optimizations and expanded UI documentation, ensuring the framework remains both performant and accessible as it scales across Web2 and Web3 environments.

## 🛠️ Key Developments

### Cloud Infrastructure & Metering
The team successfully transitioned to a more mature cloud-hosted agent model. Key updates included:
*   **Billing & Provisioning:** Enabled metered billing via signed webhook emission ([#8094](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8094)) and per-inference spend tracking ([#8089](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8089)). Agents are now isolated in dedicated containers via the sandbox registry ([#8073](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8073)).
*   **Authentication:** Resolved critical staging issues with magic-link sign-ins by adjusting CORS and CSP configurations ([#8197](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8197), [#8196](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8196)).

### Orchestrator & Agent Autonomy
Efforts focused on making agents more capable and reliable in autonomous workflows:
*   **Coding Workbench:** Launched a Claude-Code-style workbench supporting streaming and diffs, optimized for the `opencode + Cerebras` backend ([#8095](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8095)).
*   **Logic Refinements:** Improved message routing to prioritize coding tasks over web searches ([#8160](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8160)) and implemented keyless `WEB_FETCH` for non-Anthropic models ([#8097](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8097)).
*   **Stability:** Addressed race conditions in completion-deduplication to prevent duplicate replies ([#8161](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8161)).

### Desktop & UI/UX
*   **Performance:** Optimized desktop startup by introducing deferred model warmup and "tray-first" initialization ([#8151](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8151), [#8148](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8148)).
*   **UI Primitives:** Expanded Storybook documentation for nine core components and added prompt suggestions to the `ContinuousChatOverlay` to improve user onboarding ([#8200](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8200), [#8201](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8201)).

## 🐛 Issues & Triage

### Closed Issues
The team successfully resolved several high-impact blockers:
*   **Build & Runtime:** Fixed persistent build failures in the `develop` branch and regressions in agent image booting ([#8099](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8099), [#8100](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8100)).
*   **Orchestrator Logic:** Corrected JSON-RPC error reporting and false-positive freshness gates that previously hindered sub-agent performance ([#8101](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8101), [#8127](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/8127)).

### New & Active Issues
*   **Orchestrator UX:** Issue [#8162](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8162) remains a focal point, with ongoing discussions regarding sub-agent completion narration and data mangling. The community is actively debating a router-internal recovery design to replace current planner-driven respawn loops.
*   **Dependency Management:** Issues regarding `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` in bundled environments ([#8142](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8142)) highlight the ongoing challenge of managing transitive dependencies in complex workspace setups.

## 💬 Community & Collaboration
The project saw high levels of collaborative problem-solving, particularly regarding the orchestrator's routing logic. The transition from a forced planner-routing approach to a sub-agent path for `WEB_FETCH` (validated in [#8122](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/8122)) demonstrates a strong consensus-driven development process. Additionally, the ecosystem continues to grow, marked by the integration of the `@1claw/plugin-elizaos` into the third-party registry, signaling healthy external interest and contribution.
